name: Iceland (state flag, 1944–1991)

colors.symbolism: the Atlantic Ocean and the mountains of Iceland

description: The 1944–1991 state flag of Iceland — a swallow-tailed version of the national flag with the original 1944 deep cobalt blue and brighter red, prior to the colour standardization of 1991.
history: When Iceland became a republic on 17 June 1944, the existing flag designs (in use as a kingdom from 1918) were retained. The exact shades were standardized in 1991, slightly darkening the blue and adjusting the red.
